print_pattern_bank_v1.description: Print/pattern intelligence: scale, density, contrast, and mixing rules. Keeps advice friendly, non-judgy, and practical.

positive_print_harmony: This pattern reads intentional, not busy.
The scale of the print works really well on the outfit.
The pattern adds personality without taking over.
This print has a clean rhythm—easy on the eye.
The print + solid pairing is super smart here.
That pattern choice feels polished, not loud.
The print gives interest but still feels wearable.
The density is just right—doesn’t overwhelm the look.
This print feels elevated because the rest is clean.
print_scale_guidance: This print scale feels balanced with the silhouette.
A smaller-scale print would read cleaner and more minimal.
A larger-scale print would make a stronger statement—if you want bold.
This scale is working because there’s enough solid space around it.
The print isn’t fighting the outfit shape, which is perfect.
If you want it more premium, keep the print scale consistent across pieces.
Large prints look best when the rest is very simple.
Small prints look best when the silhouette is clean and not too layered.
print_density_contrast: The contrast level is controlled—so it looks polished.
The print density feels calm and wearable.
This high-contrast print works because everything else is neutral.
The print feels soft in contrast, which reads expensive.
The contrast is a bit strong, so it can steal focus quickly.
This print is dense, so keeping accessories minimal is the move.
If you want it cleaner, reduce contrast or density in one area.
A calmer contrast print would make it feel more refined.
constructive_print_clash_or_busy: It’s cute, but the prints are slightly competing.
The eye doesn’t know where to land yet—just a bit busy.
The print density is high in multiple places, so it feels crowded.
The patterns aren’t speaking the same language right now.
The contrast is strong on both pieces, so it’s fighting a little.
It’s close—one piece just needs to calm down.
The prints are both trying to be the hero at once.
The pattern mix could be cleaner with one solid anchor.
The outfit feels slightly over-styled because of competing prints.
This would look more premium with one print and one solid.
refinement_suggestions: Make one piece the hero print and keep the other solid.
Anchor the print with a neutral shoe/bag to calm it down.
If you’re mixing prints: keep one small-scale + one large-scale, and match at least one color.
If you want it premium: repeat one color (shoe/bag/top) so it feels intentional.
Reduce accessories when the print is busy—let the pattern do the talking.
If the print is high-contrast, keep the silhouette simple and clean.
Add a solid layer (jacket/overshirt) to break up the print density.
Choose one focal area for print (top OR bottom) and keep the rest quiet.
If you want minimal: swap to a lower-contrast version of the print.
Keep textures matte when prints are loud—matte makes it look expensive.

usage_rules.default_pattern: Use 1 print observation. Add 1 refinement only if user asks 'too much?' or says it feels off.
usage_rules.tone_integration: Reaction → Print observation → Optional refinement → Invitation closer.
